title: campanula glomerata subsp. salviifolia
intro: Campanula glomerata subsp. salviifolia, also known as the Clustered Bellflower, is a perennial plant belonging to the Campanulaceae family. It is native to Europe and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of Campanula glomerata subsp. salviifolia can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
